steps.title: Use the multiple-factor claim
steps.content: The author argues that pedestrian habits cannot be explained by one feature of Vancouver alone. Pedestrian amenities may matter, but demographics, commuting distance, and other factors also influence whether people walk.

steps.title: Avoid single-cause claims
steps.content: Choice A says amenities reduce walkability elsewhere, which the text never claims. Choice B treats amenities as an effect rather than a factor. Choice C ranks amenities below other influences without evidence. The remaining choice presents amenities as one of several factors. The answer is D.
